name: Head of Digital Content — Tier 1 Sports Leagues (US)
angle: Content operations leader at a professional league frustrated by slow highlight turnaround and limited social reach
why: Current US league campaigns (leagues - US - pulse) are generating near-zero delivery (2 impressions / Rs 3.67 in 7d). This tightly-scoped ABM segment targets the decision-maker most likely to own the PULSE buying decision at a league level, and has not been tested as a standalone audience.
size: Est. 8,000–15,000 (US LinkedIn, Seniority: Director+, company size 51–5000)
headline: 37,000 match moments. 51.8M views. One season.
body:
NSL automated real-time highlight delivery across 80 matches — 37K+ clips, 51.8M video views, 2× Instagram follower growth in a single season.

If your league is still clipping manually, PULSE eliminates the bottleneck. Clips live in seconds. Every sport. Every moment.

👉 See the NSL case study.
reason: leagues - US - pulse is effectively dormant (2 impressions, 7d). A fresh ICP-matched audience with a specific case-study hook will generate signal to determine whether the problem is creative, audience, or budget.

name: Head of Sponsorship & Commercial — European Sports Properties
angle: Sponsorship revenue leader who needs to prove media value to sponsors but lacks the measurement infrastructure
why: BRAND GAUGE has no dedicated active campaign. The EU Leagues campaign (Leagues - pulse - EU) has 0 clicks and 0 leads in 7d despite Rs 961.51 spend — suggesting audience or message mismatch. A sponsorship-specific segment with a commercial ROI message tests a new product angle in the same EU geography.
geo: United Kingdom, Germany, France, Netherlands, Spain
headline: Prove sponsor ROI in real time — not in a post-season deck.
body:
Sponsors demand proof of media value faster than ever. BRAND GAUGE delivers real-time sponsor analytics — AI-powered media measurement that tracks every logo exposure, calculates equivalent media value, and gives you data you can put in front of a sponsor the same day.

Stop losing renewal conversations because your measurement arrives three months late.

👉 Book a 20-minute demo.
reason: Untested product angle (BRAND GAUGE) in a geography where we already have campaign infrastructure (EU). Low incremental cost to test; high strategic value if sponsorship leaders convert.

name: CTO and Head of Technology — OTT Platforms and Streaming Services
angle: Technology decision-maker evaluating AI infrastructure for content automation and media asset management
why: The broadcaster ABM campaigns focus on content/digital roles. The CTO/tech buyer controls the build-vs-buy decision for AI highlight generation and MAM. AXIS's 100% AI tagging accuracy is a strong technical proof point that has not been tested with a technology-focused message in the US or EU.
headline: 100% AI tagging accuracy. Every asset. Zero manual QA.
body:
Media teams waste thousands of hours annually on manual asset tagging and content retrieval.

AXIS delivers AI-powered media asset management with 100% tagging accuracy — automatically organising every clip, highlight, and broadcast asset so your team can find, licence, and distribute content instantly.

Building this in-house takes years. AXIS is live in weeks.

👉 See a technical walkthrough.
reason: Untested audience persona (CTO/tech buyer) for AXIS. Differentiates from the content-leader message currently running. Technical proof points (100% accuracy, fast deployment) are highly credible to this segment.

name: Digital & Social Media Directors — European Football Federations
angle: Pain-led: league federations struggling with manual content production at scale across multi-country competitions
why: Current EU campaigns target broad 'leagues' but miss the specific decision-maker: Digital/Social Directors at national football, basketball, and volleyball federations. These individuals control content budgets and feel the daily pain of covering 100+ matches with small teams. Not currently being tested in any active campaign.
geo: United Kingdom, Germany, France, Spain, Italy, Netherlands
headline: Your Highlights Editor Costs Hours. AI Costs Seconds.
body: NSL automated 37,000 match clips across 80 games — 51.8M views, 2× Instagram growth, zero extra headcount. PULSE AI publishes your league's highlights within seconds of the final whistle, fully formatted for every social platform. Built for federations who compete for fan attention 365 days a year.
reason: High-intent segment untested in current campaigns. EU Leagues campaign (Leagues - pulse - EU) is under-delivering severely (-60.4% WoW) and targeting appears too broad. Narrowing to Digital Directors at federations should improve CTR and lead quality.

name: VP / SVP of Content & Programming — US Regional Sports Networks
angle: Outcome-led: RSN executives under pressure to produce more content with shrinking editorial budgets
why: The US broadcaster campaign (broadcasters & OTT - US - ABM) is spending Rs 13,336/week with 0 leads and a declining CTR. The ABM approach needs a complementary persona-based layer targeting content decision-makers at regional sports networks who are actively seeking cost reduction and audience growth solutions post cord-cutting disruption.
headline: 500% More Video Views. Same Production Budget.
body: HockeyOne's broadcast team delivered 10.6M video views across 7 weeks — 500% Facebook video view surge, 308% YouTube growth — all automated by PULSE AI. No added editors. No overnight cuts. Just real-time highlights published the moment they happen. See what PULSE can do for your network in a 20-minute live demo.
reason: Distinct from current ABM campaign which targets accounts, not personas. Adding a persona-based layer to the US broadcaster strategy creates a two-pronged approach: ABM for warm accounts, persona targeting for net-new decision-makers. Addresses the zero-lead crisis with a fresh, untested segment.

name: Chief Revenue Officer / Sponsorship Directors — Sports Properties
angle: ROI-led: sponsorship and revenue teams unable to prove media value to sponsors, risking renewal
why: BRAND GAUGE is completely absent from all active campaigns. Zero budget is being spent on sponsorship analytics despite it being a core product. CROs and Sponsorship Directors at leagues, teams, and events are a high-value segment with a clear pain point: proving ROI to sponsors at contract renewal time. This is a net-new revenue opportunity for Spectatr.ai.
geo: United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Australia
headline: Prove Your Sponsors' ROI Before They Ask.
body: Sponsorship deals are won and renewed on data — but most rights holders are still manually clipping footage and guessing at media value. BRAND GAUGE gives you AI-powered sponsor analytics and real-time media value measurement across every platform. Show your sponsors exactly what they got. Keep the deal. Grow the contract.
reason: BRAND GAUGE has zero campaign presence in current active campaigns — a complete gap in the go-to-market. CROs and Sponsorship Directors represent a high-LTV segment with budget authority and urgent renewal-driven pain. This audience tests an entirely new product line and buyer persona.

name: Multi-Sport Event Directors — Major Games Organising Committees
angle: Operational scale: generating highlights and content for 50+ sports simultaneously is an unsolved problem. ISG case study (512 daily highlights, 50K+ clips) is the proof point.
why: Islamic Solidarity Games case study (57 NOC committees, 512 daily highlights, 7.5M+ views) is a perfect match for organising committees running multi-sport events. This audience is completely untested and sits outside the current Leagues/Teams/Broadcaster targeting. Job titles include Chief Digital Officer, Director of Broadcasting, Head of Content — all active on LinkedIn. Budget cycles are event-driven, making them high-intent when event is 12-18 months out.
headline: 512 highlights a day. 57 countries. One AI system.
body:
At the Islamic Solidarity Games Riyadh 2025, Spectatr published 512 daily highlights across 57 NOC committees — 50,000+ real-time clips over 15 days, 7.5M+ video views.

If you're organising a multi-sport event, you already know the content problem. Dozens of venues, thousands of moments, zero time to edit.

We solve it automatically.

→ Talk to us about your next Games.
reason: Zero current spend against this persona. ISG case study provides direct, undeniable social proof. Event planning timelines mean high purchase intent. PULSE + AXIS bundle creates higher ACV opportunity vs single-product campaigns.

name: Head of Production & Post-Production — Tier 2/3 Sports Broadcasters
angle: Production cost and team efficiency: Table Tennis England saved 250+ hours in 3 months with a 5-person team. Regional broadcasters face the same constraint at larger scale.
why: Current AXIS campaign targets Broadcasters & OTT broadly. This sub-segment focuses specifically on the production operations buyer — the person who owns the post-production workflow, headcount, and turnaround SLAs. They are distinct from the digital/content buyer and respond to efficiency and cost metrics rather than engagement metrics. Table Tennis England case study (250+ hours saved, 3× upload increase, +105% total views) resonates strongly with this persona.
geo: UK, Australia, Germany, France, Netherlands, USA
headline: 250+ hours saved in 3 months. Same team. More content.
body:
Table Tennis England had a 5-person team producing 5 videos a week. After AXIS AI tagging, they publish 15 — and they reclaimed 250+ hours in 3 months.

Total views jumped 105%. Views per video grew 65%.

If your production team is the bottleneck, the problem isn't people. It's the workflow.

→ See how AXIS fits into your pipeline.
reason: Distinct buyer persona from current broad Broadcaster targeting. Efficiency/cost angle differentiates from engagement-focused PULSE messaging. Table Tennis England case study provides direct, relatable proof. Estimated CPL improvement vs current AXIS campaign based on tighter persona-message fit.

name: VP Fan Engagement & Digital Experience — Professional Sports Teams (Top 5 Leagues)
angle: Fan retention and time-spent: JORDY AI's 30% M1 retention boost and 140% time-spent increase are metrics this persona is directly accountable for. Current JORDY AI campaign targets Sports Teams broadly — this narrows to the VP/Director-level digital buyer at clubs in top-tier leagues.
why: JORDY AI campaign is already the best performer (CTR 0.81%, CPL $90.38) but targets Sports Teams broadly. Narrowing to the VP Digital/Fan Experience persona at clubs in top-5 global leagues (Premier League, NBA, NFL, LaLiga, Bundesliga tier) should improve CPL further by increasing message-persona fit. This buyer owns app engagement, second-screen experience, and fan retention metrics — exactly what JORDY AI moves. The 140% time-spent and 30% M1 retention numbers are board-level KPIs for these clubs.
headline: Fans open the app. Then leave. Here's the fix.
body:
The average sports fan spends 4 minutes in a club app before churning.

Clubs using Jordy AI — Spectatr's fan engagement agent — see 30% better first-month retention and 140% more time spent per session.

Jordy serves personalised highlights, real-time moments, and interactive content — automatically, for every fan, at scale.

60% of users discover content they'd never have found manually.

→ See Jordy in action.
reason: Builds on JORDY AI's strongest-performing campaign (CTR 0.81%, CPL $90.38) with tighter persona targeting. Narrower audience + stronger message-fit = expected CPL reduction below $90. High ACV accounts (top-tier clubs) justify premium CPL even if it rises. Untested at this segmentation level.

name: Multi-Sport Federation Digital Directors — Global
why: Multi-sport federations (Olympic committees, continental games, regional associations) have the highest clip volume requirements and the largest potential PULSE contract values. ISG Riyadh 2025 (50,000+ clips, 57 NOCs) is a direct peer-reference for this audience. Currently untested as a standalone LinkedIn segment — likely bundled into a broader 'Leagues' audience, which dilutes relevance and CPL efficiency.
headline: 50,000 AI clips. 57 countries. 15 days. Is your federation next?
body:
At the Islamic Solidarity Games Riyadh 2025, PULSE generated 50,000+ real-time highlights for 57 National Olympic Committees — 512 published daily, 7.5 million views generated.

No editors. No delays. Every moment, every nation, in seconds.

If you’re managing a multi-sport event in 2026 or beyond, this is the benchmark for AI-powered content at scale.

👉 See the full ISG case study.
reason: Multi-sport federations are the highest-value PULSE buyers (event-scale contracts, multi-year deals). ISG proof point creates instant credibility. Estimated CPL on LinkedIn for this segment: $80–150 (high intent, niche audience). Segment is untested and should be isolated from the broader Leagues audience for clean CPL benchmarking.

name: OTT Platform Content & Technology Leaders — Europe & APAC
angle: Broadcast reliability + volume — PULSE as infrastructure
why: OTT platforms (regional sports streamers, telco sports products, rights-holder OTT) are evaluating AI highlights infrastructure for 2026–2027 rights cycles. Their buyer is the VP of Content or CTO, and their objection is reliability at scale, not feature lists. This is a distinct segment from 'Broadcasters' and should be tested separately. No evidence this segment is currently targeted.
geo: Europe (UK, DACH, Nordics), Asia-Pacific (Australia, SE Asia)
headline: Real-time AI highlights, built for OTT scale. No editors. No delays.
body:
OTT audiences expect match highlights within seconds of the moment — not minutes, not hours.

PULSE delivers: AI-generated clips, published automatically, across 15+ sports.

At the Islamic Solidarity Games: 512 highlights/day. 50,000+ clips. 15 days. Zero manual intervention.

Built for the volume your platform demands.

👉 Book a 20-minute technical demo.
reason: OTT rights cycles are accelerating in Europe and APAC. AI highlights infrastructure decisions are being made now for 2026–2027. 'Book a Demo' CTA suits a technical evaluator buyer (CTO/VP) better than a content download. Segment isolation from general 'Broadcasters' allows CPL benchmarking by buyer type.

name: Sports Club CMOs & Heads of Digital — Professional Teams (UK, Australia, MENA)
angle: Fan retention + content ROI — JORDY AI + PULSE bundle
why: Professional sports clubs (football, rugby, basketball, cricket) have a clear digital KPI stack: M1 retention, time-in-app, content engagement. JORDY AI’s 30% M1 retention and 140% time-spent metrics map directly to this KPI stack. Simultaneously, PULSE’s real-time clips solve the content volume problem that drives poor retention. A bundled JORDY+PULSE message for club CMOs is currently untested and addresses two pain points in one narrative.
geo: United Kingdom, Australia, UAE, Saudi Arabia
headline: 30% more fans stayed after Month 1. Here’s what changed.
body:
Most sports clubs publish great content.

Most fans still churn within 30 days.

The problem isn’t the content — it’s the distribution. Fans aren’t seeing the moments that matter to them.

Jordy AI fixes that: serving the right content to the right fan, automatically.

Result: 30% M1 retention boost. 140% increase in time spent. +60% new content discovered.

Same content. Smarter delivery.

👉 See how Jordy works for clubs like yours.
reason: UK, Australia, and MENA are Spectatr’s strongest existing case study markets (HockeyOne = Australia, NSL = Australia, ISG = MENA). Geographic relevance increases ad resonance and CTR. Club CMOs in these markets are actively investing in fan engagement tech for 2026 season. Bundle angle (JORDY + PULSE) creates cross-sell opportunity from a single lead.
